title: Remove admin-only restriction — allow any user with username and password to access appointments and feedback dashboard

requirements.text: Remove the admin-only access restriction from the dashboard. Any user who provides a non-empty username and password should be granted access to view the appointments and feedback sections, without requiring special admin credentials or role checks.

quotes: Remove admin access all user with username and password can access appointment and feedback

acceptanceCriteria: The login form accepts any non-empty username and password combination
No admin role check or hardcoded admin credentials are enforced on the frontend
After logging in with any non-empty username/password, the user sees the appointments and feedback tabs
The AdminLoginForm component no longer gates access based on admin role verification

requirements.text: Remove or bypass any backend admin role checks that restrict reading appointments and feedback data, so that any authenticated session (any username/password) can retrieve and view submitted appointments and feedback.

quotes: Remove admin access all user with username and password can access appointment and feedback

acceptanceCriteria: The backend no longer rejects calls to view appointments and feedback based on admin role
Any caller (not just admin) can query submitted appointments and feedback entries
Existing appointment and feedback submission flows continue to work as before

constraints: Do not remove the username and password login UI — access still requires entering credentials, just no admin role restriction
Do not break existing appointment booking and feedback submission flows
Do not modify immutable frontend paths (hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, hooks/useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui)
nonGoals: Adding new authentication mechanisms (e.g., Internet Identity)
Changing the visual design or layout of the dashboard
Adding or removing tabs from the admin dashboard
